## Classes

### <a name="slurm"></a>`slurm`

You can then specialize the various aspects of the configuration,
for instance

        class { 'slurm':
            ensure => 'present'
        }

=== Authors

The UL HPC Team <hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu> of the University of Luxembourg, in
particular
* Sebastien Varrette <Sebastien.Varrette@uni.lu>
* Valentin Plugaru   <Valentin.Plugaru@uni.lu>
* Sarah Peter        <Sarah.Peter@uni.lu>
* Hyacinthe Cartiaux <Hyacinthe.Cartiaux@uni.lu>
* Clement Parisot    <Clement.Parisot@uni.lu>

License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
== Class: slurm::config::plugstack

This PRIVATE class handles the Slurm SPANK configuration file i.e. 'plugstack.conf'

Documentation: https://slurm.schedmd.com/spank.html

SPANK provides a very generic interface for stackable plug-ins which may be
used to dynamically modify the job launch code in Slurm. SPANK plugins may be
built without access to Slurm source code. They need only be compiled against
Slurm's spank.h header file, added to the SPANK config file plugstack.conf,
and they will be loaded at runtime during the next job launch

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::config::plugstack` class:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--config--plugstack--ensure)

##### <a name="-slurm--config--plugstack--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Boolean`

Should the spank config file be present

Default value: `true`

### <a name="slurm--munge"></a>`slurm::munge`

Time-stamp: <Fri 2024-08-23 17:13:19 hcartiaux>

File::      <tt>munge.pp</tt>
Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2017 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Class: slurm::munge

MUNGE (MUNGE Uid 'N' Gid Emporium) is an authentication service for creating
and validating credentials. It is designed to be highly scalable for use in an
HPC cluster environment.
 It allows a process to authenticate the UID and GID of another local or
remote process within a group of hosts having common users and groups. These
hosts form a security realm that is defined by a shared cryptographic key.
Clients within this security realm can create and validate credentials without
the use of root privileges, reserved ports, or platform-specific methods.

For more information, see https://github.com/dun/munge

This Puppet class is responsible for setting up a working Munge environment to
be used by the SLURM daemons.

See https://slurm.schedmd.com/authplugins.html

/!\ We assume the RPM 'slurm-munge' has been already installed -- this class
does not care about it

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::munge` class:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--munge--ensure)
* [`create_key`](#-slurm--munge--create_key)
* [`daemon_args`](#-slurm--munge--daemon_args)
* [`gid`](#-slurm--munge--gid)
* [`key_content`](#-slurm--munge--key_content)
* [`key_filename`](#-slurm--munge--key_filename)
* [`key_source`](#-slurm--munge--key_source)
* [`uid`](#-slurm--munge--uid)
* [`service_manage`](#-slurm--munge--service_manage)

##### <a name="-slurm--munge--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of the Munge service - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--munge--create_key"></a>`create_key`

Data type: `Boolean`

Default: true
Whether or not to generate a new key if it does not exists

Default value: `$slurm::params::munge_create_key`

##### <a name="-slurm--munge--daemon_args"></a>`daemon_args`

Data type: `Array`

Default: []
Set the content of the DAEMON_ARGS variable, which permits to set
additional command-line options to the daemon. For example, this can
be used to override the location of the secret key (--key-file) or
set the number of worker threads (--num-threads) See
https://github.com/dun/munge/wiki/Installation-Guide#starting-the-daemon

Default value: `$slurm::params::munge_daemon_args`

##### <a name="-slurm--munge--gid"></a>`gid`

Data type: `Integer`

Default: 992
GID of the munge group

Default value: `$slurm::params::munge_gid`

##### <a name="-slurm--munge--key_content"></a>`key_content`

Data type: `Optional[Variant[String,Binary]]`

Default: undef
The desired content of the munge key file, as a string or binary object.
This attribute is mutually exclusive with source and target.

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--munge--key_filename"></a>`key_filename`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/etc/munge/munge.key'
The secret key filename

Default value: `$slurm::params::munge_key`

##### <a name="-slurm--munge--key_source"></a>`key_source`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Default: undef
A source file, which will be copied into place on the local system.
This attribute is mutually exclusive with content.
The normal form of a puppet: URI is
        puppet:///modules/<MODULE NAME>/<FILE PATH>

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--munge--uid"></a>`uid`

Data type: `Integer`

Default: 992
UID of the munge user

Default value: `$slurm::params::munge_uid`

##### <a name="-slurm--munge--service_manage"></a>`service_manage`

Data type: `Boolean`



Default value: `$slurm::service_manage`

##### <a name="-slurm--pam--allowed_users"></a>`allowed_users`

Default: []

Default: see 'templates/pam_slurm.erb'
Content of /etc/pam.d/slurm

Default value: `$slurm::params::pam_content`

##### <a name="-slurm--pam--ulimits"></a>`ulimits`

Data type: `Hash`

Default:  {
            'memlock' => 'unlimited',
            'stack'   => 'unlimited',
            'nproc'   => 10240,
           }

Default value: `$slurm::params::ulimits`

##### <a name="-slurm--pam--ulimits_source"></a>`ulimits_source`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Source file for /etc/security/limits.d/80_slurm.conf

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--pam--use_pam_slurm_adopt"></a>`use_pam_slurm_adopt`

Data type: `Boolean`

Default: false
Whether or not use the pam_slurm_adopt  module (to Adopt incoming
connections into jobs) -- see
https://github.com/SchedMD/slurm/tree/master/contribs/pam_slurm_adopt

Default value: `$slurm::params::use_pam_slurm_adopt`

##### <a name="-slurm--pam--configfile"></a>`configfile`

Data type: `String`



Default value: `$slurm::params::pam_configfile`

### <a name="slurm--pmix"></a>`slurm::pmix`

Time-stamp: <Wed 2019-10-09 11:02 svarrette>

File::      <tt>pmix.pp</tt>
Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2019 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
== Class: slurm::pmix

This facility class handles the installation of PMIx.
It is meant to be a simple wrapper around the slurm::pmix::* definitions

#### Examples

##### install version 3.2.3 of PMIx

```puppet

slurm::pmix { '3.1.4':
   ensure => 'present',
   builddir => "/root/rpmbuild/",
}
```

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::pmix` class:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--pmix--ensure)
* [`version`](#-slurm--pmix--version)
* [`srcdir`](#-slurm--pmix--srcdir)
* [`checksum_type`](#-slurm--pmix--checksum_type)
* [`checksum`](#-slurm--pmix--checksum)
* [`builddir`](#-slurm--pmix--builddir)
* [`do_build`](#-slurm--pmix--do_build)
* [`do_package_install`](#-slurm--pmix--do_package_install)
* [`src_checksum`](#-slurm--pmix--src_checksum)
* [`src_checksum_type`](#-slurm--pmix--src_checksum_type)

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `String`

Default: 'present'
Ensure the presence (or absence) of building

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--version"></a>`version`

Data type: `String`

Default: 3.1.4' (see params.pp)
PMIx version to install

Default value: `$slurm::params::pmix_version`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--srcdir"></a>`srcdir`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/usr/local/src'
Target directory for the downloaded sources

Default value: `$slurm::params::srcdir`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--checksum_type"></a>`checksum_type`

Data type: `String`

Default: 'sha1'
archive file checksum type (none|md5|sha1|sha2|sh256|sha384| sha512).

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--checksum"></a>`checksum`

Data type: `String`

Default: see params.pp
archive file checksum (match checksum_type)

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--builddir"></a>`builddir`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/root/rpmbuild' on redhat systems
Top directory of the sources builds (i.e. RPMs, debs...)
For instance, built RPMs will be placed under
<builddir>/RPMS/${::architecture}

Default value: `$slurm::params::builddir`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--do_build"></a>`do_build`

Data type: `Boolean`

Default: true
Do we perform the build of the OpenPMIx packages from sources or not?

Default value: `$slurm::params::do_build`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--do_package_install"></a>`do_package_install`

Data type: `Boolean`

Default: true
Do we perform the install of the OpenPMIx packages or not?

Default value: `$slurm::params::do_package_install`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--src_checksum"></a>`src_checksum`

Data type: `String`



Default value: `$slurm::params::pmix_src_checksum`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--src_checksum_type"></a>`src_checksum_type`

Data type: `String`



Default value: `$slurm::params::pmix_src_checksum_type`

### <a name="slurm--repo"></a>`slurm::repo`

Time-stamp: <Thu 2022-06-30 14:39 svarrette>

File::      <tt>repo.pp</tt>
Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2017 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Class: slurm::repo

This class takes care of the control repository for the slurm configuration, which
version your slurm configuration files.
For many reasons, we decided NOT to directly version the /etc/slurm directory,
but rather to have a control repository cloned in another directory and
following a specific layout depicted below:

* for the cluster '${clustername}', a root directory named'${clustername}'
  hosts all common slurm configuration files
* a script '${syncscript}' (typically 'bin/commit_slurm_configs.sh') is
  responsible for synchronizing the configuration and commit it into this

  .
  ├── bin/
  |    └── commit_slurm_configs.sh
  └── <clustername>/
         ├── cgroup.conf
         ├── gres.conf
         ├── plugstack.conf
         ├── plugstack.conf.d
         │   └── x11.conf
         ├── slurm.conf
         └── topology.conf


Also, this server is expected to have push writes on the branch 'server/${::hostname}'

# Create the SSH key, using for instance the maestrodev/ssh_keygen module
ssh_keygen { $slurm::username:
   comment => "${slurm::username}@${::fqdn}",
   home    => $slurm::params::home,
 }
# /!\ Render the host key known globally using sshkey { ... }
# Now you can expect the clone to work
class { 'slurm::repo':
    ensure => 'present',
    source => 'ssh://git@gitlab.uni.lu:8022/ULHPC/slurm.git',
 }

#### Examples

##### Clone the SLURM control repository into '/usr/local/src/git/ULHPC/slurm'

```puppet

```

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::repo` class:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--repo--ensure)
* [`force`](#-slurm--repo--force)
* [`provider`](#-slurm--repo--provider)
* [`basedir`](#-slurm--repo--basedir)
* [`path`](#-slurm--repo--path)
* [`source`](#-slurm--repo--source)
* [`branch`](#-slurm--repo--branch)
* [`linkdir`](#-slurm--repo--linkdir)
* [`link_subdir`](#-slurm--repo--link_subdir)
* [`syncscript`](#-slurm--repo--syncscript)

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent', 'latest']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of the repository - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--force"></a>`force`

Data type: `Boolean`

Default: force
Specifies whether to delete any existing files in the repository path
if creating a new repository. Use with care.

Default value: `false`

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--provider"></a>`provider`

Data type: `Enum['git', 'bzr', 'hg', 'svn']`

Default: 'git'
Specifies the backend to use for this vcsrepo resource.
Valid options: 'bzr', 'git', 'hg', and 'svn'.

Default value: `'git'`

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--basedir"></a>`basedir`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/usr/local/src/git'
Base directory where to clone the repository

Default value: `$slurm::params::repo_basedir`

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--path"></a>`path`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Default: ''
Specifies a location for the managed repository.
It this stays empty, the repository will be cloned under $basedir/$namespace/$reponame

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--source"></a>`source`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Specifies a source repository to serve as the upstream for your managed repository.
Typically a Git repository URL or a hash of remotename => URL mappings

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--branch"></a>`branch`

Data type: `String`

Default: 'HEAD'

## Defined types

### <a name="slurm--acct--account"></a>`slurm::acct::account`

Time-stamp: <Thu 2017-09-07 15:30 svarrette>

File::      <tt>acct/account.pp</tt>
Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2017 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Defines: slurm::acct::account

This definition takes care of adding (or removing) a Slurm bank account,
typically specified at job submit time using the --account= option. These may
be arranged in a hierarchical fashion, for example accounts chemistry and
physics may be children of the account science. The hierarchy may have an
arbitrary depth.

/!\ WARNING: this assumes you are using the MySQL plugin as SlurmDBD plugin
and a working SLURMDBD.
The name of this resource is expected to be the qos name.

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::acct::account` defined type:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--acct--account--ensure)
* [`cluster`](#-slurm--acct--account--cluster)
* [`options`](#-slurm--acct--account--options)

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--account--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of the entity - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--account--cluster"></a>`cluster`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Default: 'cluster'
Cluster name on which the specified resources are to be available

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--account--options"></a>`options`

Data type: `Hash`

Default: {}
Specification options -- see https://slurm.schedmd.com/sacctmgr.html
Elligible keys: Clusters=, DefaultQOS=, Description=, Fairshare=,
                GrpTRESMins=, GrpTRES=, GrpJobs=, GrpMemory=,
                GrpNodes=, GrpSubmitJob=, GrpWall=, MaxTRESMins=,
                MaxTRES=, MaxJobs=, MaxNodes=, MaxSubmitJobs=,
                MaxWall=, Names=, Organization=, Parent=,
                and QosLevel=

Default value: `{}`

= Defines: slurm::acct::cluster

This definition takes care of adding (or removing) a cluster to the slurm database.
/!\ WARNING: this assumes you are using the MySQL plugin as SlurmDBD plugin and a working SLURMDBD.
The name of this resource is expected to be the cluster name.

  }

TODO/ from the manpage, we can provide the following options to this definition

```puppet
slurm::acct::cluster { 'thor':
ensure    => 'present',
```

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--acct--cluster--ensure)
* [`options`](#-slurm--acct--cluster--options)

Ensure the presence (or absence) of the entity - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--cluster--options"></a>`options`

Data type: `Hash`

Default: {}
Specification options -- see https://slurm.schedmd.com/sacctmgr.html

                   ControlHost, ControlPort, DefaultQOS,
                   Fairshare, Flags, GrpTRESMins, GrpTRES GrpJobs,
                   GrpMemory, GrpNodes, GrpSubmitJob, MaxTRESMins,
                   MaxTRES, MaxJobs, MaxNodes, MaxSubmitJobs,
                   MaxWall, NodeCount, PluginIDSelect, RPC, TRES

= Defines: slurm::acct::mgr

Generic wrapper for all sacctmgr commands
See https://slurm.schedmd.com/sacctmgr.html

/!\ WARNING: this assumes you are using the MySQL plugin as SlurmDBD plugin
and a working SLURMDBD.

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::acct::mgr` defined type:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--acct--mgr--ensure)
* [`entity`](#-slurm--acct--mgr--entity)
* [`value`](#-slurm--acct--mgr--value)
* [`content`](#-slurm--acct--mgr--content)
* [`options`](#-slurm--acct--mgr--options)

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--mgr--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present','absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of the cluster - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--mgr--entity"></a>`entity`

Data type: `Slurm::Entity`

the entity to consider for the name of this resource.
Elligible values in [ 'account',
                      'association',
                      'cluster',
                      'coordinator',
                      'event',
                      'job',
                      'qos',
                      'Resource',
                      'RunawayJobs',
                      'stats',
                      'transaction',
                      'user',
                      'wckeys', ]

Default value: `''`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--mgr--value"></a>`value`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Value to associate to the entity. Uses $name by default

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--mgr--content"></a>`content`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Default: ''
Eventual content as a string for the specifications of the entity.
You probably SHOULDN'T mix this with the options

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--mgr--options"></a>`options`

Data type: `Optional[Hash]`

Default: {}
Specification options, preferably as a hash -- depends on the entity
See https://slurm.schedmd.com/sacctmgr.html

Default value: `undef`

### <a name="slurm--acct--qos"></a>`slurm::acct::qos`

Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2017 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Defines: slurm::acct::qos

This definition takes care of adding (or removing) a Quality of Service (QOS)
for each job submitted to Slurm.
For more details: see <https://slurm.schedmd.com/qos.html>

/!\ WARNING: this assumes you are using the MySQL plugin as SlurmDBD plugin
and a working SLURMDBD.
The name of this resource is expected to be the qos name.

slurm::acct::qos { 'qos-interactive':
  ensure    => 'present',
  priority  => 20,
  options   => {
    preempt  => 'qos-besteffort',
    grpnodes => 30,
  }
}

this will run the following command:
  sacctmgr -i add qos qos-interactive Priority=20 Preempt=qos-besteffort Grpnodes=30

#### Examples

##### Adding the qos 'qos-interactive' to Slurm

```puppet

```

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::acct::qos` defined type:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--acct--qos--ensure)
* [`priority`](#-slurm--acct--qos--priority)
* [`options`](#-slurm--acct--qos--options)
* [`content`](#-slurm--acct--qos--content)

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--qos--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of the entity  - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--qos--priority"></a>`priority`

Data type: `Integer`

Default: 0

Default value: `0`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--qos--options"></a>`options`

Data type: `Hash`

Default: {}
Specification options -- see https://slurm.schedmd.com/sacctmgr.html
Elligible keys:  Description=, Flags=, GraceTime=, GrpJobs=,
                 GrpSubmitJob=, GrpTRES=, GrpTRESMins=, GrpWall=,
                 MaxJobs=, MaxSubmitJobsPerUser=, MaxTRESMins=,
                 MaxTRESPerJob=, MaxTRESPerNode=, MaxTRESPerUser=,
                 MaxWall=, Names=, Preempt=, PreemptMode=,
                 Priority=, UsageFactor=, and UsageThreshold=

Default value: `{}`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--qos--content"></a>`content`

Data type: `Optional[String]`



Default value: `undef`

### <a name="slurm--acct--user"></a>`slurm::acct::user`

Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2017 UL HPC Team

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Defines: slurm::acct::user

This definition takes care of adding (or removing) a login name into Slurm.
Only lowercase usernames are supported.

/!\ WARNING: this assumes you are using the MySQL plugin as SlurmDBD plugin
and a working SLURMDBD.
The name of this resource is expected to be the qos name.

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::acct::user` defined type:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--acct--user--ensure)
* [`$defaultaccount`](#-slurm--acct--user---defaultaccount)
* [`options`](#-slurm--acct--user--options)
* [`defaultaccount`](#-slurm--acct--user--defaultaccount)

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--user--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of the entity - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--user---defaultaccount"></a>`$defaultaccount`

Data type: `String`

Identify the default bank account name to be used for a job if none is
specified at submission time.

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--user--options"></a>`options`

Data type: `Hash`

Default: {}
Specification options -- see https://slurm.schedmd.com/sacctmgr.html
Elligible keys:

Default value: `{}`

##### <a name="-slurm--acct--user--defaultaccount"></a>`defaultaccount`

Data type: `Optional[String]`



Default value: `undef`

### <a name="slurm--build"></a>`slurm::build`

Time-stamp: <Thu 2022-06-30 14:27 svarrette>

File::      <tt>build.pp</tt>
Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2017 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Defines: slurm::build

This definition takes care of building Slurm sources into RPMs using 'rpmbuild'.
It expect to get as resource name the SLURM version to build
This assumes the sources have been downloaded using slurm::download


This will typically run the following rpmbuild command:

    rpmbuild -ta --with hdf5 --with hwloc --with lua --with mysql --with numa --with pmix \
             --define "_topdir /root/rpmbuild" --define "_with_pmix --with-pmix=/usr" \
             /usr/local/src/slurm-19.05.3-2.tar.bz2

and thus produce the following RPMs under /root/rpmbuild/RPMS/x86_64/:

     slurm-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-contribs-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-devel-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-example-configs-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-libpmi-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-openlava-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-pam_slurm-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-perlapi-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-slurmctld-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-slurmd-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-slurmdbd-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm
     slurm-torque-19.05.3-2.el7.x86_64.rpm

#### Examples

##### Building version 19.05.3-2 of SLURM

```puppet

slurm::build { '19.05.3-2':
   ensure => 'present',
   srcdir => '/usr/local/src',
   dir    => '/root/rpmbuild',
   with   => [ 'hdf5', 'hwloc', lua', 'mysql', 'numa', 'pmix' ]
}
```

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::build` defined type:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--build--ensure)
* [`srcdir`](#-slurm--build--srcdir)
* [`dir`](#-slurm--build--dir)
* [`with`](#-slurm--build--with)
* [`without`](#-slurm--build--without)

##### <a name="-slurm--build--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of building - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--build--srcdir"></a>`srcdir`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/usr/local/src'
Where the [downloaded] Slurm sources are located

Default value: `$slurm::params::srcdir`

##### <a name="-slurm--build--dir"></a>`dir`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/root/rpmbuild' on redhat systems
Top directory of the sources builds (i.e. RPMs, debs...)
For instance, built RPMs will be placed under
<dir>/RPMS/${::architecture}

Default value: `$slurm::params::builddir`

##### <a name="-slurm--build--with"></a>`with`

Data type: `Array`

Default: [ 'lua', ... ] -- see slurm::params
see https://github.com/SchedMD/slurm/blob/master/slurm.spec
List of --with build options to pass to rpmbuild

Default value: `$slurm::params::build_with`

##### <a name="-slurm--build--without"></a>`without`

Data type: `Array`

Default: [] -- see slurm::params
see https://github.com/SchedMD/slurm/blob/master/slurm.spec
List of --without build options to pass to rpmbuild

Default value: `$slurm::params::build_without`

### <a name="slurm--download"></a>`slurm::download`

}

#### Examples

##### Downloading version 19.05.3-2 of SLURM

```puppet

slurm::download { '19.05.3-2':
   ensure        => 'present',
   checksum      => '6fe2c6196f089f6210d5ba79e99b0656f5a527b4',
   checksum_type => 'sha1',
   target        => '/usr/local/src/',
```

* [`checksum`](#-slurm--download--checksum)
* [`checksum_type`](#-slurm--download--checksum_type)
* [`checksum_verify`](#-slurm--download--checksum_verify)

##### <a name="-slurm--download--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`



Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--download--target"></a>`target`

Data type: `String`



Default value: `$slurm::params::srcdir`

##### <a name="-slurm--download--checksum"></a>`checksum`

Data type: `Optional[String]`



Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--download--checksum_type"></a>`checksum_type`

Data type: `String`



Default value: `$slurm::params::src_checksum_type`

Data type: `Boolean`

### <a name="slurm--install--packages"></a>`slurm::install::packages`

Time-stamp: <Wed 2020-09-23 20:42 svarrette>

File::      <tt>install/packages.pp</tt>
Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2017 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Defines: slurm::install::packages

This definition takes care of installing the Slurm packages, typically built
from slurm::build, for a given version passed as resource name.

#### Examples

##### install version 19.05.3-2 for a head (controller) node running also the SlurmDBD:

```puppet

slurm::install::packages { '19.05.3-2':
   ensure    => 'present',
   pkgdir    => "/root/rpmbuild/',
   slurmctld => true,
   slurmd    => true,
}
```

##### install version 19.05.3-2 for a compute node (aimed at running SLURMd)

```puppet

slurm::install::packages { '19.05.3-2':
   ensure => 'present',
   pkgdir => "/root/rpmbuild/',
   slurmd => true,
}
```

##### install version 19.05.3 for a login node:

```puppet
slurm::install::packages { '19.05.3-2':
   ensure => 'present',
   pkgdir => "/root/rpmbuild/',
}
```

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::install::packages` defined type:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--install--packages--ensure)
* [`pkgdir`](#-slurm--install--packages--pkgdir)
* [`slurmd`](#-slurm--install--packages--slurmd)
* [`slurmctld`](#-slurm--install--packages--slurmctld)
* [`slurmdbd`](#-slurm--install--packages--slurmdbd)
* [`packages`](#-slurm--install--packages--packages)
* [`wrappers`](#-slurm--install--packages--wrappers)

##### <a name="-slurm--install--packages--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of building - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--install--packages--pkgdir"></a>`pkgdir`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/root/rpmbuild' on redhat systems
Top directory of the sources builds (i.e. RPMs, debs...)
For instance, built RPMs will be placed under
<dir>/RPMS/${::architecture}

Default value: `$slurm::params::builddir`

##### <a name="-slurm--install--packages--slurmd"></a>`slurmd`

Data type: `Boolean`

Default: false
Install Slurmd packages (compute and head node)

Default value: `$slurm::params::with_slurmd`

##### <a name="-slurm--install--packages--slurmctld"></a>`slurmctld`

Data type: `Boolean`

Default: false
Install Slurmctrld (controller node)

Default value: `$slurm::params::with_slurmctld`

##### <a name="-slurm--install--packages--slurmdbd"></a>`slurmdbd`

Data type: `Boolean`

Default: false
Install Slurm DBD Node

Default value: `$slurm::params::with_slurmdbd`

##### <a name="-slurm--install--packages--packages"></a>`packages`

Data type: `Optional[Array]`

(optional)
Exhaustive list of the packages (RPM basenames - without versions and
os specific suffixes.) to install

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--install--packages--wrappers"></a>`wrappers`

Data type: `Optional[Array]`



Default value: `undef`

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Defines: slurm::plugin

This definition takes care of managing SLURM plugins.

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::plugin` defined type:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--plugin--ensure)
* [`path`](#-slurm--plugin--path)
* [`content`](#-slurm--plugin--content)
* [`source`](#-slurm--plugin--source)
* [`target`](#-slurm--plugin--target)

##### <a name="-slurm--plugin--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of building - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--plugin--path"></a>`path`

Data type: `Stdlib::Absolutepath`

Target directory for the downloaded sources - Default '/usr/local/src'

Default value: `'/usr/local/src'`

##### <a name="-slurm--plugin--content"></a>`content`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Default: undef
The desired contents of a topology file, as a string. This attribute is
mutually exclusive with topology_source and topology_target.

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--plugin--source"></a>`source`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Default: undef
A source file, which will be copied into place on the local system.
This attribute is mutually exclusive with {content,target}.

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--plugin--target"></a>`target`

Data type: `Optional[Stdlib::Absolutepath]`

Default: undef
Target link path

Default value: `undef`

### <a name="slurm--pmix--build"></a>`slurm::pmix::build`

Time-stamp: <Tue 2019-10-08 23:08 svarrette>

File::      <tt>pmix/build.pp</tt>
Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2019 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Defines: slurm::pmix::build

This definition takes care of building PMIx sources into RPMs using 'rpmbuild'.
It expect to get as resource name the PMIx version to build
This assumes the sources have been downloaded using slurm::pmix::download


  }

NOTE: on purpose, this definition will build separate RPMs in ${dir}/RPMS/${::architecture}:
 1. pmix-<version>-*.rpm: the main package
 2. pmix-libmpi-<version>-*.rpm: PMI-1 and PMI-2 compatibility libraries (i.e.
 libpmi and libpmi2 libraries) that provide the respective APIs and a copy of
 the PMIx library –  each API is translated into its PMIx equivalent. This
 package conflicts with slurm-libpmi, which provides its own, incompatible
 versions of libpmi.so and libpmi2.so.

In particular, ONLY THE FIRST RPM will be installed (to avoid the conflict
with slurm-libmpi).

#### Examples

##### Building version 3.1.4 (latest at the time of writing) of PMIx

```puppet

slurm::pmix::build { '3.1.4':
  ensure  => 'present',
  srcdir  => '/usr/local/src',
  dir     => '/root/rpmbuild',
  defines => [ 'install_in_opt 1' ],
```

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::pmix::build` defined type:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--pmix--build--ensure)
* [`srcdir`](#-slurm--pmix--build--srcdir)
* [`dir`](#-slurm--pmix--build--dir)
* [`defines`](#-slurm--pmix--build--defines)

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--build--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of building - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--build--srcdir"></a>`srcdir`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/usr/local/src'
Where the [downloaded] Slurm sources are located

Default value: `$slurm::params::srcdir`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--build--dir"></a>`dir`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/root/rpmbuild' on redhat systems
Top directory of the sources builds (i.e. RPMs, debs...)
For instance, built RPMs will be placed under
<dir>/RPMS/${::architecture}

Default value: `$slurm::params::builddir`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--build--defines"></a>`defines`

Data type: `Array`

Default: []
Extra options that can be passed in via rpmbuild's --define option,
knowing that the options '_topdir' and 'build_all_in_one_rpm' are
already set by this definition.
Note that --define takes *1* argument i.e. a multi-token string where
the first token is the name of the variable to define, and all
remaining tokens are the value. Pass each such string as elements of
this array.

Default value: `[]`

### <a name="slurm--pmix--download"></a>`slurm::pmix::download`

Time-stamp: <Thu 2022-06-30 14:42 svarrette>

File::      <tt>pmix/download.pp</tt>
Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2019 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Defines: slurm::pmix::download

This definition takes care of downloading PMIx sources sources for a given version
(passed as name to this resource) and placing them into $target directory
according to the guidelines provided on
https://pmix.org/support/how-to/slurm-support/#building

Latest PMIx releases: https://github.com/openpmix/openpmix/releases

https://github.com/openpmix/openpmix/releases/tag/v3.1.4):

    slurm::pmix::download { '3.1.4':
       ensure        => 'present',
       checksum      => '0f3f575e486d8492441c34276d1d56cbb48b4c37',
       checksum_type => 'sha1',
       target        => '/usr/local/src/',
    }

#### Examples

##### install version 3.1.4 (see

```puppet

```

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::pmix::download` defined type:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--pmix--download--ensure)
* [`target`](#-slurm--pmix--download--target)
* [`checksum_type`](#-slurm--pmix--download--checksum_type)
* [`checksum_verify`](#-slurm--pmix--download--checksum_verify)
* [`checksum`](#-slurm--pmix--download--checksum)
* [`url`](#-slurm--pmix--download--url)

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--download--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of building - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--download--target"></a>`target`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/usr/local/src'
Target directory for the downloaded sources

Default value: `$slurm::params::srcdir`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--download--checksum_type"></a>`checksum_type`

Data type: `String`

Default: 'sha1'
archive file checksum type (none|md5|sha1|sha2|sh256|sha384| sha512).

Default value: `$slurm::params::pmix_src_checksum_type`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--download--checksum_verify"></a>`checksum_verify`

Data type: `Boolean`

Default: false
whether checksum will be verified (true|false).

Default value: `false`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--download--checksum"></a>`checksum`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Default: ''
archive file checksum (match checksum_type)

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--download--url"></a>`url`

Data type: `Optional[String]`



Default value: `undef`

### <a name="slurm--pmix--install"></a>`slurm::pmix::install`

Time-stamp: <Wed 2021-03-31 00:59 svarrette>

File::      <tt>pmix/install.pp</tt>
Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2019 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Defines: slurm::pmix::install

This definition takes care of installing the PMIx packages typically built
from the slurm::pmix::build (to ensure *separate* RPMs builds have been
produced to avoid conflicts with slurm-libpmi, which provides its own,
incompatible versions of libpmi.so and libpmi2.so.), for a given version for
PMIx passed as resource name.

#### Examples

##### install version 3.1.4 (latest at the time of writing) of PMIx

```puppet

slurm::pmix::install { '3.1.4':
   ensure   => 'present',
   builddir => "/root/rpmbuild/",
}
```

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::pmix::install` defined type:

* [`ensure`](#-slurm--pmix--install--ensure)
* [`builddir`](#-slurm--pmix--install--builddir)

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--install--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`

Ensure the presence (or absence) of installation - Default: 'present'

Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

##### <a name="-slurm--pmix--install--builddir"></a>`builddir`

Data type: `String`

Default: '/root/rpmbuild/' on redhat systems
Top directory of the sources builds (i.e. RPMs, debs...)
For instance, built RPMs will be placed under
<builddir>/RPMS/${::architecture}

Default value: `$slurm::params::builddir`

### <a name="slurm--repo--syncto"></a>`slurm::repo::syncto`

Time-stamp: <Thu 2022-06-30 14:42 svarrette>

File::      <tt>repo/syncto.pp</tt>
Author::    UL HPC Team (hpc-sysadmins@uni.lu)
Copyright:: Copyright (c) 2017 UL HPC Team
License::   Apache-2.0

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
= Define: slurm::repo::syncto

This definition takes care of synchronizing the control repository of the
slurm configuration (which is cloned using the 'slurm::repo' class) toward a
directory (typically a shared mountpoint)

#### Parameters

The following parameters are available in the `slurm::repo::syncto` defined type:

* [`subdir`](#-slurm--repo--syncto--subdir)
* [`target`](#-slurm--repo--syncto--target)
* [`purge`](#-slurm--repo--syncto--purge)
* [`ensure`](#-slurm--repo--syncto--ensure)

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--syncto--subdir"></a>`subdir`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Specifies a source [sub]directory within the repository holding the
sources we wish to synchronize

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--syncto--target"></a>`target`

Data type: `Optional[String]`

Target [shared] directory where to synchronize

Default value: `undef`

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--syncto--purge"></a>`purge`

Data type: `Boolean`

Default: false
If set, rsync will use '--delete'

Default value: `false`

##### <a name="-slurm--repo--syncto--ensure"></a>`ensure`

Data type: `Enum['present', 'absent']`



Default value: `$slurm::params::ensure`

## Data types

### <a name="Slurm--Entity"></a>`Slurm::Entity`

The Slurm::Entity data type.

Alias of `Enum['account', 'association', 'cluster', 'coordinator', 'event', 'job', 'qos', 'Resource', 'RunawayJobs', 'stats', 'transaction', 'user', 'wckeys']`
